pr.title: Match 13 misc functions (gr, it, ft, gm, if)
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Matched or substantially improved a batch of miscellaneous fighter, item, stage, game-mode, and interface functions, replacing placeholder decomp markers with C implementations, adding several recovered prototypes and small struct/field recoveries, and adding one scoped MWCC dont_inline pragma for soundtest.

key_files.role: implements fn_800F98F4 for Kirby Luigi-copy fireball spawning and muzzle effect; adds itluigifireball include
implements fn_80122B54 for Blizzard landing transition and slope foot rotation
implements fn_80168A6C as a CSS data copy helper using a temporary local source struct and flat output buffer writes
recovers fields in lbl_80472D28_t, adds RegClearEv, and implements fn_8017FE54 grade display fill computation
updates fn_8017FE54 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void fn_8017FE54(HSD_GObj*)
implements grHomeRun_8021EA30 distance computation and adds Home-Run Contest data declarations and language include
updates grHomeRun_8021EA30 prototype to void grHomeRun_8021EA30(f32*)
implements grRCruise_80200B48 with a 17-entry initialization loop for Rainbow Cruise moving platform entries
updates grRCruise_80200B48 prototype to void grRCruise_80200B48(Ground_GObj*)
implements grYorster_80202428 and recovers YorsterParams pointer typing for cloud-platform behavior
updates grYorster_80202428 prototype with concrete parameter types
adds grYorster_TrackElement and expands grYorster_GroundVars with elements[9]
implements un_802FFCD0 as an array fill loop and scopes it with #pragma dont_inline on/reset
recovers additional itKyasarinAttributes float fields used by Chansey collision logic
implements Ice Climbers ice block physics and collision handlers
implements it_802F0F6C bomb projectile spawn and ownership/linking setup; replaces placeholder include with db/db.h
updates it_802F0F6C prototype with concrete owner, position, item kind, and facing parameters
implements Chansey collision/turnaround boundary logic
implements it_802EB5C8 Tingle reset and balloon height calculation; adds lb_00B0 and MSL math includes
updates it_802EB6DC prototype to void it_802EB6DC(Item_GObj*)

what_changed: Replaced placeholder markers for 13 named functions across gr, it, ft, gm, and if code with C implementations listed in the PR body.
Updated several headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures for recovered functions.
Recovered small local or file-scope struct fields used by new implementations, including gmregclear event data, Yorster ground variables, and itKyasarinAttributes floats.
Added a scoped #pragma dont_inline around un_802FFCD0 to prevent MWCC from inlining it into callers, according to the PR body.
Used loops in grRCruise_80200B48, grYorster_80202428, and un_802FFCD0 instead of repeated copied blocks.
Used project helpers/macros such as GET_ITEM, ABS, HSD_JObjGetTranslationY, lb_8000B1CC, Item_80268B18, and Fighter_ChangeMotionState in the recovered code.

smart_moves.text: The Rainbow Cruise initialization was expressed as a natural for-loop over 17 entries, matching the repeated platform-entry pattern without copying 17 blocks.
The Sound Test helper was expressed as a simple counted array fill loop, with the pragma tightly scoped to the single function.
Function bodies were accompanied by header prototype updates where signatures were recovered.
Stage and item variable access used recovered typed fields or temporary structs rather than leaving every access as opaque pointer arithmetic.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a repeated initialization pattern indexes a data table and a GroundVars array, try a counted loop first; grRCruise_80200B48 initializes 17 moving-platform entries this way.
Small recovered struct overlays can make flat data-copy or event-data routines reviewable while preserving exact offset access when broader type names are not yet known.
When a function body proves its parameter and return types, update the owning header in the same PR rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Use existing engine helpers and macros for clear source-level intent where they fit the recovered operation, such as ABS for absolute velocity/distance and GET_ITEM for item user-data access.

naming_conventions.name: Address-style unknown function names retained
naming_conventions.text: Most newly implemented functions kept address-style names such as fn_800F98F4, fn_80122B54, fn_80168A6C, fn_8017FE54, and it_802F0F6C rather than inventing unsupported semantic names.

naming_conventions.name: Short local role names
naming_conventions.text: Recovered functions use common short local role names such as fp, ip, gp, gobj, pos, attr, and sa when the object role is clear.

naming_conventions.name: Offset-style fields retained where semantics are not proven
naming_conventions.text: Newly recovered structs use offset-style fields such as x14, x1C, x20, x30, and x34 when the PR does not provide stronger semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: un_802FFCD0 is wrapped in #pragma dont_inline on/reset; the PR body says this prevents MWCC from inlining it into callers.
PAD_STACK is used narrowly in fn_800F98F4, fn_80122B54, and grHomeRun_8021EA30.
fn_80168A6C casts arg0 to an inline local struct with x0/x4/x8/xC fields to express source layout while writing arg1 as a flat s32 buffer.
Loop forms are used for a 17-entry stage initialization, a 9-entry Yorster element search, and a counted soundtest array fill.
Several recovered implementations were matched with owning-header signature updates in the same diff.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported +384 matched-code bytes, with 2 new 100% matches: un_802FFCD0 and it_802EB5C8.
The bot also reported 16 improvements in unmatched items and 2 regressions in unmatched data sections: gmregclear .sdata2 and itclimbersice .sdata2.
No human review comments were present in the supplied PR slice.

follow_up_queries: gmregclear fn_8017FE54 RegClearEv lbl_80472D28 x10C x110
itKyasarinAttributes xC x30 x34 boundary turnaround
decomp-dev bot PR2326 .sdata2 regressions gmregclear itclimbersice

notes: The PR body claims all functions are 100% matched via objdiff, but the loaded bot report from shortly after PR creation lists only 2 new 100% matches and many functions as 95–99% improvements, plus two .sdata2 regressions.
The slice has no human review comments, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be inferred.
The diff supports changed files, implemented function bodies, header updates, pragma use, and local typing changes, but not broader global standards beyond existing loaded standards.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use natural loops for indexed stage/item initialization patterns
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2326 provides a concrete example in grRCruise_80200B48: 17 similar moving-platform initialization records are represented as a for-loop over entries and an ID table rather than hand-unrolled code.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep address-style names when behavior is recovered but semantic names are not source-backed
accepted_candidate_records.text: Several recovered functions remained address-style, while local variables use role names only where clear. This is consistent with conservative naming and avoids inventing semantic global names from behavior summaries alone.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Function recovery should update owning headers when signatures are proven
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2326 pairs multiple recovered function bodies with header updates from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures.

source_update_candidates.title: Yorster GroundVars gained a 9-element track/cloud element array
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2326 adds struct grYorster_TrackElement with x4 and x1C fields and appends elements[9] to grYorster_GroundVars; grYorster_80202428 searches these elements for item_gobj and increments x4 by value.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path/type fact for stage GroundVars layout; should be reviewed against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: itKyasarinAttributes recovered boundary/timer float fields used by rolling patrol collision
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2326 changes itKyasarinAttributes to expose xC, x30, and x34 as f32 fields; itKyasarin_UnkMotion2_Coll uses x30/x34 as facing-dependent X thresholds and stores xC into kyasarin item var x24 on turn-around.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential item attribute layout fact; semantic names are not proven by this PR and should remain offset-style unless stronger evidence exists.

source_update_candidates.title: Scoped dont_inline pragma exception must include inlining rationale
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2326 states that #pragma dont_inline was added around un_802FFCD0 to prevent MWCC from inlining it into callers. This supports documenting pragma exceptions only when tightly scoped and justified by matching behavior.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards already discourage pragmas except as tightly scoped exceptions; curator may decide whether this PR is useful as an example, not as a new broad permission.

source_update_candidates.title: Bot report conflicts with PR body match claim
source_update_candidates.text: PR body says all functions 100% match via objdiff, while the loaded decomp-dev bot report lists only un_802FFCD0 and it_802EB5C8 as new 100% matches and reports the rest as partial improvements plus two .sdata2 regressions.
source_update_candidates.reason: Curator should avoid promoting the all-functions-100%-matched claim unless later verification evidence outside this slice resolves the conflict.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR-body statement 'All functions 100% match via objdiff' as an accepted validation result from this slice because the loaded bot report conflicts with it.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no review comments or reviews are present.
Do not treat PAD_STACK or dont_inline use in this PR as general style permission; loaded standards require narrow evidence for such tactics.
Do not assign semantic field names to xC, x30, x34, x1C, x20, or similar recovered fields from this PR alone.
